justification: All 5 expected rules identified and exceeded (8 total). State machine directionality correctly modeled with in_transit->pending forbidden (BR-4) and delivered terminal (BR-5). All 13 acceptance criteria use Given/When/Then format. Testable assertions are concrete and developer-actionable. Weight bracket rules correctly marked invariant: false with clear justification. Confidence level of 90 is at the upper boundary of the 65-90 range, but assumptions are stated and reasonable.

observations.text: Exceeded minimum rule count (8 vs 5 required), splitting weight validation into separate >0 and <=1000 rules and detailing all three bracket boundaries individually
All weight bracket rules (BR-6, BR-7, BR-8) correctly classified as invariant: false with clear reasoning that thresholds are business pricing policy
State machine directionality explicitly modeled: in_transit->pending forbidden (BR-4) and delivered terminal (BR-5)
13 acceptance criteria all in proper Given/When/Then format with concrete preconditions and expected outcomes
Testable assertions are specific and actionable, e.g., 'A shipment with weight 9.99 kg must be classified as Standard; a shipment with weight 10 kg must NOT be Standard'
Edge cases section identifies boundary values, skip transitions, and idempotent re-delivery — goes beyond minimum requirements
AC-13 captures the inferred rule that Pending cannot skip directly to Delivered, demonstrating deep domain reasoning
Confidence level at 90 is at the ceiling of the acceptable range; given the noted assumption about Pending->Delivered not being explicitly stated, 80-85 would better reflect the uncertainty
Assumptions are clearly stated in confidence section, including linear state progression inference and address format gaps
